channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Machine Learning Analysis of Serum Metabolomics for Multi-Disease Classification and Proteome-Metabolome Pathway Insights in T2D, IBD, and CRC.
item.description: Journal of proteome research - Type 2 diabetes (T2D), inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and colorectal cancer (CRC) share overlapping metabolic alterations that hinder early, disease-specific diagnosis. Using publicly available ...

item.title: Development and validation of a predictive model for early failure of biological therapy in perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease based on three-dimensional transrectal ultrasound.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Despite the clinical use of three-dimensional transrectal ultrasound (3D-TRUS) in pfCD, no validated model exists to predict early biologic treatment failure. Our objective was to develop and valid...

item.title: Urinary Calprotectin Is Not a Reliable Surrogate of Fecal Calprotectin Levels in Adults and Children with Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Fecal calprotectin (FCAL) is an established biomarker for monitoring intestinal inflammation in inflammatory bowel disease. However, fecal sampling can be socially unacceptable and inconvenient. Ur...

item.title: Identifying omic biomarkers for chronic inflammatory diseases associated with periodontitis using percolation on multi-disease gene co-expression networks.
item.description: Communications medicine - Chronic inflammatory diseases, such as ulcerative colitis (UC), Crohn's disease (CD), Alzheimer's disease (AD) and Parkinson's disease (PD) are clinically related to periodontitis. However, the com...

item.title: Integrated RNA-seq and scRNA-seq to explore the biological mechanisms of mitophagy-related genes in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: PloS one - Mitophagy's role in ulcerative colitis (UC) is not fully understood. This study explores mitophagy's impact on UC and aims to create a diagnostic model. The transcriptomic datasets of patients with...

item.title: Refractory ulcerative colitis patient keeping complete remission with drug-free after hematopoietic stem cell transplantation for ileocecal diffuse large B-cell lymphoma.
item.description: Clinical journal of gastroenterology -

item.title: Comorbidities of Atopic Dermatitis: Emerging Evidence and Clinical Considerations.
item.description: Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology - Review - Atopic dermatitis (AD) is the most common chronic inflammatory skin diseases and has traditionally been viewed as a disorder confined to the skin. However, emerging epidemiologic, genetic, and mech...

item.title: A JAK of two trades: beneficial or adverse effects of Janus kinase inhibition for plasma LDL-cholesterol and major adverse cardiovascular events in inflammatory bowel disease patients?
item.description: Current opinion in lipidology - Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors have emerged as potent anti-inflammatory drugs and are prescribed in rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, cardiovascular safety con...

item.title: Psychological distress links to magnetic resonance enterography abnormalities in Crohn's disease.
item.description: Insights into imaging - Emerging evidence underscores psychological distress as a potential modifier of clinical outcomes in Crohn's disease (CD), though its etiological basis remains undetermined. This study investigated...

item.title: Magnetic Resonance Enterography Predicts Long-Term Outcomes in Crohn's Disease: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: JGH open : an open access journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - Review - Crohn's disease (CD) is characterized by chronic transmural inflammation, which often leads to bowel damage. While mucosal healing (MH) is an established treatment target, transmural healing (TH) h...

item.title: Red Cell Distribution Width as an Independent Marker of Objective Disease Activity in Ulcerative Colitis and Crohn's Disease: A Large Real-World Cohort of 1000 Patients.
item.description: The Turkish journal of gastroenterology : the official journal of Turkish Society of Gastroenterology - The treat-to-target (T2T) strategy requires objective biomarkers in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Red cell distribution width (RDW) is of interest and routinely available; however, its incremen...

item.title: Prevalence, incidence and mortality of pediatric inflammatory bowel disease in Catalonia (EPIBDCat).
item.description: Revista espanola de enfermedades digestivas - The epidemiologic status of pediatric inflammatory bowel disease (PIBD) has not been widely studied in Western Europe. The prevalence and incidence trends of the disease are largely unknown. The ai...

item.title: Utility of T2* and DWI-IVIM to distinguish inflammatory and noninflammatory strictures in Crohn's disease: a prospective cross-sectional cohort study.
item.description: European radiology experimental - In Crohn's disease (CD), noninflammatory strictures benefit from surgical resection or endoscopic dilatation, whereas inflammatory strictures might still respond to anti-inflammatory treatment. The...

item.title: Therapy Persistence and Dose Escalation of Advanced Treatments in First and Second Line of Ulcerative Colitis and Crohn's Disease - A Retrospective Cohort Analysis of Healthcare Claims Data.
item.description: Zeitschrift fur Gastroenterologie - There is limited data on advanced therapy outcomes in second-line vs. first-line advanced treatment of patients with ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD). We conducted a retrospective h...

item.title: Inflammatory bowel disease and Primary sclerosing cholangitis: immunological links and clinical outlook.
item.description: Expert review of clinical immunology - Review - Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) represents a progressive fibroinflammatory cholangiopathy intricately linked to inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), forming a distinctive overlap syndrome driven ...

item.title: Shared Genetics Implicate Gut Microbiota and Immunity in Anterior Uveitis and Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Ocular immunology and inflammation - Anterior uveitis (AU) is a common extraintestinal manifestation of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). This study investigates the shared genetic architecture and pleiotropic loci between AU and IBD....

item.title: Demographic and Clinical Insights Into Pyoderma Gangrenosum: A Retrospective Cross-Sectional Study From a Tertiary Care Hospital.
item.description: Health science reports - Pyoderma gangrenosum (PG) is a rare, non-infectious inflammatory skin disease often associated with systemic disorders such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), malignancies, and autoimmune conditi...

item.title: Causal relationship between autoimmune diseases and iron deficiency anemia: a two-sample mendelian randomization study.
item.description: Scientific reports - Iron deficiency anemia (IDA) is a major contributor to global morbidity and disease burden. While observational studies have suggested associations between autoimmune diseases (ADs) and IDA, the ca...

item.title: Factors Associated With Lack of Pre-Operative Stoma Site Marking Before Ostomy Surgery.
item.description: The Journal of surgical research - Preoperative stoma marking is recommended for patients undergoing potential ostomy surgery. Factors influencing marking are unknown. The purpose of this study was to identify factors associated wit...

item.title: Practices and perceptions of imaging assessment in inflammatory bowel disease: a nationwide survey of physicians in Korea by KASID Guidelines Taskforce Team.
item.description: Intestinal research - Imaging-based assessment is essential for evaluating disease activity and structural damage in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). This study aimed to evaluate real-world use and perceptions of imagi...
